title: API and Coordinates
text: sassy_search() searches . sassy_searcher() creates reusable searcher. sassy_searcher_search() searches object. sassy_features() prints backend/build information. sassy_set_backend() selects backend first use. sassy_fastx_iter() opens chunked FASTA/FASTQ iterator. sassy_fastx_next() returns next record-count-bounded batch.

title: API and Coordinates
text: Coordinates 0-based half-open. text_start, text_end: interval text. pattern_start, pattern_end: interval pattern. pattern_idx, text_idx: input index vector/list inputs.
code: matches <- sassy_search(list("ACGT"), list("TTACGTAA"), k = 0, alphabet = "dna", rc = FALSE) matches[, c("text_start", "text_end", "pattern_start", "pattern_end", "cigar")] #> <sassy_matches> 1 match #> text_start text_end pattern_start pattern_end cigar #>          2        6             0           4    4=

title: API and Coordinates
text: pattern text lists sequence elements. element may raw vector non-missing character scalar. keeps one raw vector one sequence bytes lets callers mix raw bytes, ordinary strings, ALTREP-backed raw elements input list. FASTA/FASTQ iterator returns batches already shaped API: batch$seq list raw ALTREP sequence elements batch$id ALTREP character vector suitable text_id.
code: sassy_search(list(charToRaw("ACGT")), list(charToRaw("TTACGTAA")), k = 0, alphabet = "dna") #> <sassy_matches> 2 matches #> pattern_idx text_idx text_start text_end pattern_start pattern_end cost strand cigar #>           0        0          2        6             0           4    0      +    4= #>           0        0          2        6             0           4    0      -    4= fq <- tempfile(fileext = ".fastq") writeLines(c("@r1", "ACGT", "+", "!!!!"), fq, useBytes = TRUE) batch <- sassy_fastx_next(sassy_fastx_iter(fq, batch_records = 1)) sassy_search(list("ACG"), batch$seq, k = 0, alphabet = "dna", rc = FALSE, text_id = batch$id) #> <sassy_matches> 1 match #> pattern_idx text_idx text_id text_start text_end pattern_start pattern_end cost strand cigar #>           0        0      r1          0        3             0           3    0      +    3=

title: API and Coordinates
text: match_region = TRUE adds matched text interval. Reverse-strand regions reverse-complemented match pattern direction.
code: sassy_search(   list("ATCGATCG"),   list("GGGGATCGATCGTTTT"),   k = 1,   alphabet = "dna",   match_region = TRUE ) #> <sassy_matches> 3 matches #> pattern_idx text_idx text_start text_end pattern_start pattern_end cost strand  cigar match_region #>           0        0          2       10             0           8    1      -   7=1X     ATCGATCC #>           0        0          4       12             0           8    0      +     8=     ATCGATCG #>           0        0          6       14             0           8    1      - 1=1X6=     AACGATCG

title: API and Coordinates
text: default strategy = "pairwise" searches pattern/text pair independently. general path works mixed pattern lengths alphabets. strategies performance-oriented paths call different Sassy kernels: batch_texts: one pattern, multiple texts per batch. batch_patterns: multiple equal-length patterns per batch. encoded_patterns / v2: Sassy encoded-pattern path. batch_patterns encoded_patterns use Sassy’s multi-pattern encoding. sassy 0.2.1 encoding implemented IUPAC profile equal byte-length patterns.

title: Backend Selection
text: backend = "auto" uses first installed supported backend order: wasm_simd128 avx512 avx2 neon scalar
code: sassy_features() #> <sassy_features> #> dispatch: dynamic #> selected backend: avx2 #> installed backends: scalar, avx2, avx512 #> supported backends: scalar, avx2 #> CPU: avx2=yes avx512f=no neon=no #> Rust backend: avx2 (native_simd=yes)

title: Backend Selection
text: Call sassy_set_backend() Rsassy native call. Backend loading one-shot, test another backend new R process.
code: script <- tempfile(fileext = ".R") writeLines(c(   "library(Rsassy)",   "sassy_set_backend('scalar')",   "print(sassy_features())" ), script) cat(system2(file.path(R.home("bin"), "Rscript"), c("--vanilla", script), stdout = TRUE), sep = "\n") #> <sassy_features> #> dispatch: dynamic #> selected backend: scalar #> installed backends: scalar, avx2, avx512 #> supported backends: scalar, avx2 #> CPU: avx2=yes avx512f=no neon=no #> Rust backend: portable_scalar (native_simd=no) unlink(script)

title: Backend Selection
text: sassy crate uses build-time SIMD configuration SIMD-dependent types. R package therefore builds separate Rust libraries supported backend families instead switching individual functions runtime.

title: Chunked FASTA/FASTQ Iteration
text: Pass batch$seq directly text list use batch$id explicit metadata. Call sassy_fastx_next() repeatedly returns NULL.
code: sassy_search(   list("ACG"),   batch$seq,   k = 0,   alphabet = "dna",   rc = FALSE,   text_id = batch$id ) #> <sassy_matches> 3 matches #> pattern_idx text_idx text_id text_start text_end pattern_start pattern_end cost strand cigar #>           0        0   read1          0        3             0           3    0      +    3= #>           0        0   read1          4        7             0           3    0      +    3= #>           0        1   read2          4        7             0           3    0      +    3= sassy_fastx_next(it) #> NULL

title: Chunked FASTA/FASTQ Iteration
text: Quality strings optional Rsassy search use . Set include_qual = FALSE avoid retaining quality bytes batch. FASTA input, batch$qual always NULL. Wrapped FASTA sequence lines copied batch sequence slab stripping \r \n. avoids calling needletail’s per-record seq() allocation path wrapped FASTA.
code: it_no_qual <- sassy_fastx_iter(fq, batch_records = 2, include_qual = FALSE) batch_no_qual <- sassy_fastx_next(it_no_qual) is.null(batch_no_qual$qual) #> [1] TRUE fa <- tempfile(fileext = ".fa") writeLines(c(">seq1", "AC", "GT", ">seq2", "TTTT"), fa, useBytes = TRUE) fa_batch <- sassy_fastx_next(sassy_fastx_iter(fa, batch_records = 10)) as.character(fa_batch$id) #> [1] "seq1" "seq2" rawToChar(fa_batch$seq[[1]]) #> [1] "ACGT" is.null(fa_batch$qual) #> [1] TRUE

title: Chunked FASTA/FASTQ Iteration
text: Gzip-compressed FASTA/FASTQ files supported vendored needletail gzip backend. Plain gzip input still sequential. Parallel random access ordinary fastq.gz require separate auxiliary index cache part iterator.
code: fq_gz <- tempfile(fileext = ".fastq.gz") con <- gzfile(fq_gz, open = "wb") writeLines(readLines(fq, warn = FALSE), con, useBytes = TRUE) close(con)  gz_batch <- sassy_fastx_next(sassy_fastx_iter(fq_gz, batch_records = 10)) as.character(gz_batch$id) #> [1] "read1" "read2"

title: Chunked FASTA/FASTQ Iteration
text: iterator record-count bounded batch_records. call sassy_fastx_next() parses many records stores IDs, sequences, optional qualities native slab buffers plus offset/length arrays. uncompressed input gzip input alike, Rsassy avoids materializing sequence strings R. hot path : gzip, decompression unavoidable, additional R character-vector materialization sequences. read-raw access, Rsassy obtains pointer native slab Rust worker threads start. Writable raw-vector access gets private R-owned copy, user mutation change shared batch buffer. local synthetic checks used development, needletail record iteration allocated handful times FASTQ counting. Slab batches avoided per-record Vec allocations scaled peak memory batch_records; example, batches 10k, 50k, 100k, 250k short reads used progressively larger bounded slabs similar parse time. numbers hardware- file-dependent, package tests assert semantics rather benchmark times.
code: needletail record -> native batch slabs -> raw ALTREP slices -> Rsassy byte slices

title: Chunked FASTA/FASTQ Iteration
text: Tinytest coverage exercises: FASTQ batching record count; gzip FASTQ input; wrapped FASTA input CR/LF stripping slab path; include_qual = FALSE; direct search batch$seq text_id = batch$id; UTF-8 record ID round-tripping; extracted ALTREP IDs/raw slices surviving parent batch object removed garbage collection runs; writable raw-vector access copying instead mutating shared slab; rejecting non-iterator external pointers passed sassy_fastx_next(). package check also rebuilds offline vendored Rust bundle runs tinytest suite R CMD check.

title: Getting Started with Rsassy
code: install.packages(   "Rsassy",   repos = c("https://sounkou-bioinfo.r-universe.dev", "https://cloud.r-project.org") )

title: Getting Started with Rsassy
text: result sassy_matches data frame. Coordinates 0-based half-open.
code: library(Rsassy)  sassy_search(list("ATCGATCG"), list("GGGGATCGATCGTTTT"), k = 1, alphabet = "dna") #> <sassy_matches> 3 matches #> pattern_idx text_idx text_start text_end pattern_start pattern_end cost strand  cigar #>           0        0          2       10             0           8    1      -   7=1X #>           0        0          4       12             0           8    0      +     8= #>           0        0          6       14             0           8    1      - 1=1X6=

title: Getting Started with Rsassy
code: searcher <- sassy_searcher("dna", rc = TRUE) sassy_searcher_search(searcher, list("ATCGATCG"), list("GGGGATCGATCGTTTT"), k = 1) #> <sassy_matches> 3 matches #> pattern_idx text_idx text_start text_end pattern_start pattern_end cost strand  cigar #>           0        0          2       10             0           8    1      -   7=1X #>           0        0          4       12             0           8    0      +     8= #>           0        0          6       14             0           8    1      - 1=1X6=

title: Getting Started with Rsassy
text: List inputs search every pattern every text. list element may raw vector non-missing character scalar, callers can mix byte strings, regular strings, ALTREP-backed raw elements. pattern_idx text_idx identify input indices.
code: sassy_search(   list("ATG", charToRaw("TTT")),   list("CCCCATGCCCCTTT"),   k = 1,   alphabet = "iupac",   rc = FALSE,   strategy = "encoded_patterns" ) #> <sassy_matches> 2 matches #> pattern_idx text_idx text_start text_end pattern_start pattern_end cost strand cigar #>           0        0          4        7             0           3    0      +    3= #>           1        0         11       14             0           3    0      +    3=

title: Getting Started with Rsassy
text: sassy_fastx_iter() sassy_fastx_next() parse FASTA/FASTQ files record-count-bounded batches. Record IDs exposed ALTREP character vector sequences list raw ALTREP slices immutable native batch buffers. See vignette("fastx-iteration", package = "Rsassy") performance validation details.
code: fq <- tempfile(fileext = ".fastq") writeLines(c("@r1", "ACGT", "+", "!!!!"), fq, useBytes = TRUE) it <- sassy_fastx_iter(fq, batch_records = 1) batch <- sassy_fastx_next(it) sassy_search(list("ACG"), batch$seq, k = 0, alphabet = "dna", rc = FALSE, text_id = batch$id) #> <sassy_matches> 1 match #> pattern_idx text_idx text_id text_start text_end pattern_start pattern_end cost strand cigar #>           0        0      r1          0        3             0           3    0      +    3=

title: Development and Vendoring
text: Rust sources installed vendored bundle. Refresh Rust dependency changes: updates: Package installation runs Cargo offline mode.
code: make vendor-rust src/rust/vendor.tar.xz inst/AUTHORS inst/LICENCE.note

title: Development and Vendoring
text: make check uses two Cargo jobs default. local builds:
code: make rd       # regenerate NAMESPACE and man/*.Rd make readme   # regenerate README.md make install  # local install make test     # tinytest make check    # R CMD build + R CMD check make reports  # render committed benchmark/conformance markdown reports make clean    # remove build artifacts make CARGO_JOBS=10 check

title: Development and Vendoring
text: configure / configure.win generate src/Makevars files. src/Makevars./ src/Makevars.win.build Rust libraries. src/install.libs.R installs backend libraries backends/. cleanup removes generated files.

text: Sounkou Mahamane Toure. Author, maintainer. Ragnar Groot Koerkamp. Copyright holder. Rick Beeloo. Copyright holder.

what: R Bindings to the sassy Approximate String Matcher
title: R Bindings to the sassy Approximate String Matcher
text: R bindings Sassy R’s native C API. Results returned data frames 0-based coordinates CIGAR strings.

title: R Bindings to the sassy Approximate String Matcher
text: Install r-universe: Source installs require Cargo/rustc >= 1.91 xz. Rust crates vendored src/rust/vendor.tar.xz offline package builds. Linux, macOS, Windows, Rsassy installs multiple backend libraries possible: scalar, AVX2, AVX-512 x86_64; scalar NEON arm64. webR/WebAssembly build uses wasm SIMD128; see browser demo. Rsassy selects best installed backend supported current CPU/runtime backend first loaded.
code: install.packages(   "Rsassy",   repos = c("https://sounkou-bioinfo.r-universe.dev", "https://cloud.r-project.org") )

title: R Bindings to the sassy Approximate String Matcher
text: result sassy_matches data frame pattern_idx, text_idx, text_start, text_end, pattern_start, pattern_end, cost, strand, cigar. Coordinates 0-based half-open. Set match_region = TRUE also want matched sequence. strand == "-", match_region reverse-complemented direction input pattern CIGAR. print method can color match_region simple ANSI escape sequences, following upstream Sassy CLI sassy grep alignment legend: green matching characters, orange mismatches, blue inserted text characters, red gaps pattern characters absent text. Coloring default meant ANSI-capable interactive terminals. Reuse searcher making repeated calls: List inputs search every pattern every text. element can raw vector character scalar, also leaves room ALTREP-backed batches lists. larger batches, use threads > 1. strategy = "encoded_patterns" (alias "v2") R equivalent CLI --v2 many equal-length short patterns. batch_patterns encoded_patterns use Sassy’s multi-pattern encoding, sassy 0.2.1 implemented IUPAC equal byte-length patterns. default strategy = "pairwise" general path alphabets mixed pattern lengths. CLI-compatible orientation available sam = TRUE. formats reverse-strand match_region cigar text direction, matching upstream Sassy sassy --sam output. Chunked FASTA/FASTQ iteration available sassy_fastx_iter() sassy_fastx_next(). Batches expose record IDs ALTREP character vector sequences list raw ALTREP slices immutable native buffers, search can consume file records without first materializing sequence strings R. CRISPR guide search available -memory sequences sassy_crispr(). Guides include PAM suffix; default PAM must match exactly IUPAC matching. file-oriented colored grep, FASTA/FASTQ filtering, large command-line pipelines, use upstream Sassy CLI directly. Inspect installed build: Backend loading one-shot per R process. need benchmark debug specific backend, call sassy_set_backend() first native Rsassy call fresh Rscript process. See vignette("backend-selection", package = "Rsassy") details.
code: library(Rsassy)  sassy_search(list("ATCGATCG"), list("GGGGATCGATCGTTTT"), k = 1, alphabet = "dna") #> <sassy_matches> 3 matches #> pattern_idx text_idx text_start text_end pattern_start pattern_end cost strand  cigar #>           0        0          2       10             0           8    1      -   7=1X #>           0        0          4       12             0           8    0      +     8= #>           0        0          6       14             0           8    1      - 1=1X6= region_matches <- sassy_search(   list("ATCGATCG"),   list("GGGGATCGATCGTTTT"),   k = 1,   alphabet = "dna",   match_region = TRUE ) region_matches #> <sassy_matches> 3 matches #> pattern_idx text_idx text_start text_end pattern_start pattern_end cost strand  cigar match_region #>           0        0          2       10             0           8    1      -   7=1X     ATCGATCC #>           0        0          4       12             0           8    0      +     8=     ATCGATCG #>           0        0          6       14             0           8    1      - 1=1X6=     AACGATCG searcher <- sassy_searcher("dna") sassy_searcher_search(searcher, list("ATCGATCG"), list("GGGGATCGATCGTTTT"), k = 1) #> <sassy_matches> 3 matches #> pattern_idx text_idx text_start text_end pattern_start pattern_end cost strand  cigar #>           0        0          2       10             0           8    1      -   7=1X #>           0        0          4       12             0           8    0      +     8= #>           0        0          6       14             0           8    1      - 1=1X6= sassy_search(   list("ATG", "TTT"),   list("CCCCATGCCCCTTT"),   k = 1,   alphabet = "iupac",   rc = FALSE,   strategy = "encoded_patterns" ) #> <sassy_matches> 2 matches #> pattern_idx text_idx text_start text_end pattern_start pattern_end cost strand cigar #>           0        0          4        7             0           3    0      +    3= #>           1        0         11       14             0           3    0      +    3= sassy_search(   list("ACGA"),   list("TTTCGTTT"),   k = 0,   alphabet = "dna",   match_region = TRUE,   sam = TRUE ) #> <sassy_matches> 1 match #> pattern_idx text_idx text_start text_end pattern_start pattern_end cost strand cigar match_region #>           0        0          2        6             0           4    0      -    4=         TCGT fq <- tempfile(fileext = ".fastq") writeLines(c("@r1", "ACGT", "+", "!!!!"), fq, useBytes = TRUE) it <- sassy_fastx_iter(fq, batch_records = 1) batch <- sassy_fastx_next(it) sassy_search(list("ACG"), batch$seq, k = 0, alphabet = "dna", rc = FALSE, text_id = batch$id) #> <sassy_matches> 1 match #> pattern_idx text_idx text_id text_start text_end pattern_start pattern_end cost strand cigar #>           0        0      r1          0        3             0           3    0      +    3= sassy_crispr(list("ACGTNGG"), list("TTTACGTAGGTTT"), k = 0, rc = FALSE) #>     guide cost strand start end match_region cigar #> 1 ACGTNGG    0      +     3  10      ACGTAGG    7= sassy_features() #> <sassy_features> #> dispatch: dynamic #> selected backend: avx2 #> installed backends: scalar, avx2, avx512 #> supported backends: scalar, avx2 #> CPU: avx2=yes avx512f=no neon=no #> Rust backend: avx2 (native_simd=yes)

title: R Bindings to the sassy Approximate String Matcher
text: Common development commands repository root: make check uses CRAN-safe default two Cargo build jobs. Use make check-fast make CARGO_JOBS=10 check local multithreaded Cargo builds.
code: make vendor-rust  # refresh src/rust/vendor.tar.xz after Rust dependency changes make rd           # regenerate NAMESPACE and man/*.Rd from R/search.R make readme       # regenerate README.md from README.Rmd make install      # install the package locally make test         # run tinytest tests make check        # build and run R CMD check make reports      # render committed conformance/performance markdown reports make clean        # remove generated build artifacts

title: Print sassy match data frames — print.sassy_matches
text: x sassy_matches data frame. ... Ignored; accepted compatibility print(). color TRUE, color match_region CIGAR operation ANSI escape sequences: green matches, orange substitutions, blue inserted text, red gaps pattern bases absent text. Defaults getOption("Rsassy.coloring", FALSE).

title: Print sassy match data frames — print.sassy_matches
text: x, invisibly.

what: Format matches in SAM-compatible text direction — sassy_as_sam
title: Format matches in SAM-compatible text direction — sassy_as_sam
text: Rsassy normally follows upstream sassy TSV convention: reverse-strand match_region values reverse-complemented CIGAR strings oriented input pattern direction. sassy_as_sam() converts reverse-strand rows text direction used SAM upstream sassy --sam output.

title: Format matches in SAM-compatible text direction — sassy_as_sam
code: sassy_as_sam(x, alphabet = "dna")

title: Format matches in SAM-compatible text direction — sassy_as_sam
text: x sassy_matches data frame. alphabet Alphabet profile used search. One "dna" "iupac" x includes match_region.

title: Format matches in SAM-compatible text direction — sassy_as_sam
text: copy x reverse-strand cigar values reversed , present, reverse-strand match_region values reverse-complemented back text direction.

title: Format matches in SAM-compatible text direction — sassy_as_sam
code: sassy_as_sam(   sassy_search(list("ACGA"), list("TTTCGTTT"), 0, alphabet = "dna", match_region = TRUE),   alphabet = "dna" ) #> <sassy_matches> 1 match #> pattern_idx text_idx text_start text_end pattern_start pattern_end cost strand cigar match_region #>           0        0          2        6             0           4    0      -    4=         TCGT

title: Search CRISPR guide targets — sassy_crispr
text: sassy_crispr() R-level equivalent upstream sassy crispr workflow -memory sequences. Guides include PAM end. default, PAM must match exactly IUPAC matching, rest guide may k edits.

title: Search CRISPR guide targets — sassy_crispr
code: sassy_crispr(   guide,   text,   k,   pam_length = 3L,   allow_pam_edits = FALSE,   max_n_frac = 0.2,   rc = TRUE,   threads = 1L,   pattern_id = NULL,   text_id = NULL )

title: Search CRISPR guide targets — sassy_crispr
text: guide List guide sequences including PAM suffix. element must raw vector non-missing character scalar. text List text sequences search. element must raw vector non-missing character scalar. k Maximum edit distance searched guide sequence. allow_pam_edits = FALSE, exact-PAM filter means effectively edit threshold outside PAM. pam_length Length PAM suffix. allow_pam_edits TRUE, require exact PAM match. max_n_frac Maximum allowed fraction N bases match_region. rc TRUE, search reverse-complement targets well. threads Number worker threads request. pattern_id Optional guide/pattern identifiers. supplied, must character vector one entry per guide adds/replaces pattern_id column. Names guide inspected. text_id Optional text identifiers. supplied, must character vector one entry per text adds/replaces text_id column. Names text inspected.

title: Search CRISPR guide targets — sassy_crispr
text: data frame CLI-style columns: guide, cost, strand, start, end, match_region, cigar. pattern_id text_id supplied, mapped identifier columns included.

title: Search CRISPR guide targets — sassy_crispr
code: sassy_crispr(list("ACGTNGG"), list("TTTACGTAGGTTT"), k = 0, rc = FALSE, text_id = "chr1") #>     guide text_id cost strand start end match_region cigar #> 1 ACGTNGG    chr1    0      +     3  10      ACGTAGG    7=

what: Create a chunked FASTA/FASTQ iterator — sassy_fastx_iter
title: Create a chunked FASTA/FASTQ iterator — sassy_fastx_iter
text: sassy_fastx_iter() opens FASTA FASTQ file returns iterator yields record-count-bounded batches. Parsing performed vendored Rust needletail parser. Sequence quality data batch exposed read-raw ALTREP slices immutable native batch buffers; eagerly materialized R strings.

title: Create a chunked FASTA/FASTQ iterator — sassy_fastx_iter
code: sassy_fastx_iter(path, batch_records = 100000L, include_qual = TRUE)

title: Create a chunked FASTA/FASTQ iterator — sassy_fastx_iter
text: path Path FASTA/FASTQ file. Gzip-compressed input supported vendored needletail gzip backend. batch_records Maximum number records returned sassy_fastx_next() call. include_qual TRUE, FASTQ qualities included batch$qual. FALSE, FASTA input, batch$qual NULL.

title: Create a chunked FASTA/FASTQ iterator — sassy_fastx_iter
text: external pointer class sassy_fastx_iter.

title: Create a chunked FASTA/FASTQ iterator — sassy_fastx_iter
code: fq <- tempfile(fileext = ".fastq") writeLines(c("@r1", "ACGT", "+", "!!!!"), fq, useBytes = TRUE) it <- sassy_fastx_iter(fq, batch_records = 1) batch <- sassy_fastx_next(it) rawToChar(batch$seq[[1]]) #> [1] "ACGT"

what: Get the next FASTA/FASTQ batch — sassy_fastx_next
title: Get the next FASTA/FASTQ batch — sassy_fastx_next
text: Get next FASTA/FASTQ batch

title: Get the next FASTA/FASTQ batch — sassy_fastx_next
code: sassy_fastx_next(iter)

title: Get the next FASTA/FASTQ batch — sassy_fastx_next
text: iter iterator created sassy_fastx_iter().

title: Get the next FASTA/FASTQ batch — sassy_fastx_next
text: NULL end file, otherwise sassy_fastx_batch list id, seq, qual elements. id ALTREP character vector, seq qual ALTREP lists whose elements raw ALTREP vectors.

title: Get the next FASTA/FASTQ batch — sassy_fastx_next
code: fq <- tempfile(fileext = ".fastq") writeLines(c("@r1", "ACGT", "+", "!!!!"), fq, useBytes = TRUE) it <- sassy_fastx_iter(fq, batch_records = 1) batch <- sassy_fastx_next(it) length(batch$id) #> [1] 1

what: Report Rsassy build and CPU feature information — sassy_features
title: Report Rsassy build and CPU feature information — sassy_features
text: Returns diagnostic information selected Rsassy backend. Calling initializes native backend already loaded. rsassy_selected_backend reports runtime-selected backend. rsassy_installed_backends character vector backend libraries found package installation, rsassy_supported_backends subset supported current CPU/runtime. "auto" selection, Rsassy chooses best supported installed backend: AVX-512 AVX2 x86_64, NEON arm64, WebAssembly SIMD128 wasm, scalar otherwise. selected_* fields describe loaded Rust backend. cpu_* fields detected C shim.

title: Report Rsassy build and CPU feature information — sassy_features
code: sassy_features()

title: Report Rsassy build and CPU feature information — sassy_features
text: sassy_features list build, selected-backend, CPU/runtime feature values.

title: Report Rsassy build and CPU feature information — sassy_features
code: sassy_features() #> <sassy_features> #> dispatch: dynamic #> selected backend: avx2 #> installed backends: scalar, avx2, avx512 #> supported backends: scalar, avx2 #> CPU: avx2=yes avx512f=no neon=no #> Rust backend: avx2 (native_simd=yes)

title: Search approximate matches with 'sassy' — sassy_search
text: Convenience wrapper creates searcher, searches, returns sassy_matches data frame. Coordinates 0-based half-open.

title: Search approximate matches with 'sassy' — sassy_search
code: sassy_search(   pattern,   text,   k,   alphabet = "dna",   rc = TRUE,   alpha = NULL,   all = FALSE,   threads = 1L,   strategy = "pairwise",   pattern_id = NULL,   text_id = NULL,   match_region = FALSE,   sam = FALSE )

title: Search approximate matches with 'sassy' — sassy_search
text: pattern List raw vectors non-missing character scalars. text List raw vectors non-missing character scalars. k Maximum edit distance. alphabet Alphabet profile. One "dna", "iupac", "ascii". rc TRUE, search reverse-complement strand well supported. alpha Optional IUPAC overhang cost [0, 1]. Use NULL disable. FALSE, return usual local-minimum matches. TRUE, return every end position score <= k; can include overlapping nested candidate alignments requires strategy = "pairwise". threads Number worker threads request bulk searches. strategy Search strategy. "pairwise" searches pattern/text pair independently general default. "batch_texts" uses one text per SIMD lane. "batch_patterns" "encoded_patterns" (alias "v2") use Sassy's multi-pattern encoding, sassy 0.2.1 implemented alphabet = "iupac" equal byte-length patterns. pattern_id Optional pattern identifiers. supplied, must non-missing character vector one entry per pattern adds/replaces pattern_id column. Names pattern inspected. text_id Optional text identifiers. supplied, must non-missing character vector one entry per text adds/replaces text_id column. Names text inspected. match_region TRUE, include match_region column. Reverse-strand regions reverse-complemented region CIGAR input pattern direction. sam TRUE, format reverse-strand match_region cigar text direction used SAM upstream sassy --sam output.

title: Search approximate matches with 'sassy' — sassy_search
text: data frame 0-based indices coordinates: pattern_idx, text_idx, text_start, text_end, pattern_start, pattern_end, cost, strand, cigar. pattern_id text_id supplied, mapped identifier columns included. requested, also includes match_region. Rows ordered input text, text start/end coordinate, pattern index.

title: Search approximate matches with 'sassy' — sassy_search
code: sassy_search(list("ACGT"), list("TTACGTAA"), 0, alphabet = "dna", rc = FALSE) #> <sassy_matches> 1 match #> pattern_idx text_idx text_start text_end pattern_start pattern_end cost strand cigar #>           0        0          2        6             0           4    0      +    4=

code: sassy_searcher(alphabet = "dna", rc = TRUE, alpha = NULL)

title: Create a reusable 'sassy' searcher — sassy_searcher
text: alphabet Alphabet profile. One "dna", "iupac", "ascii". rc TRUE, search reverse-complement strand well supported. alpha Optional IUPAC overhang cost [0, 1]. Use NULL disable.

title: Create a reusable 'sassy' searcher — sassy_searcher
text: external pointer class sassy_searcher.

title: Create a reusable 'sassy' searcher — sassy_searcher
code: searcher <- sassy_searcher("dna", rc = FALSE) sassy_searcher_search(searcher, list("ACGT"), list("TTACGTAA"), 0) #> <sassy_matches> 1 match #> pattern_idx text_idx text_start text_end pattern_start pattern_end cost strand cigar #>           0        0          2        6             0           4    0      +    4=

what: Search with a reusable 'sassy' searcher — sassy_searcher_search
title: Search with a reusable 'sassy' searcher — sassy_searcher_search
text: pattern text must lists sequences. element must raw vector non-missing character scalar. Every pattern searched every text returned pattern_idx text_idx columns identify 0-based input indices. Use threads > 1 larger batches.

title: Search with a reusable 'sassy' searcher — sassy_searcher_search
code: sassy_searcher_search(   searcher,   pattern,   text,   k,   all = FALSE,   threads = 1L,   strategy = "pairwise",   pattern_id = NULL,   text_id = NULL,   match_region = FALSE,   sam = FALSE )

title: Search with a reusable 'sassy' searcher — sassy_searcher_search
text: searcher searcher created sassy_searcher(). pattern List raw vectors non-missing character scalars. text List raw vectors non-missing character scalars. k Maximum edit distance. FALSE, return usual local-minimum matches. TRUE, return every end position score <= k; can include overlapping nested candidate alignments requires strategy = "pairwise". threads Number worker threads request bulk searches. strategy Search strategy. "pairwise" searches pattern/text pair independently general default. "batch_texts" uses one text per SIMD lane. "batch_patterns" "encoded_patterns" (alias "v2") use Sassy's multi-pattern encoding, sassy 0.2.1 implemented alphabet = "iupac" equal byte-length patterns. pattern_id Optional pattern identifiers. supplied, must non-missing character vector one entry per pattern adds/replaces pattern_id column. Names pattern inspected. text_id Optional text identifiers. supplied, must non-missing character vector one entry per text adds/replaces text_id column. Names text inspected. match_region TRUE, include match_region column. Reverse-strand regions reverse-complemented region CIGAR input pattern direction. sam TRUE, format reverse-strand match_region cigar text direction used SAM upstream sassy --sam output.

title: Search with a reusable 'sassy' searcher — sassy_searcher_search
text: data frame 0-based indices coordinates: pattern_idx, text_idx, text_start, text_end, pattern_start, pattern_end, cost, strand, cigar. pattern_id text_id supplied, mapped identifier columns included. requested, also includes match_region. Rows ordered input text, text start/end coordinate, pattern index.

what: Select the Rsassy native backend — sassy_set_backend
title: Select the Rsassy native backend — sassy_set_backend
text: Select backend current R process. Backend loading intentionally one-shot: selected shared library fixed lifetime R process. must called first native Rsassy operation, including sassy_features(), sassy_searcher(), sassy_search(). Rsassy unload replace backend DLLs reliable across R platforms. Use benchmarking installed backends separate fresh R processes.

title: Select the Rsassy native backend — sassy_set_backend
code: sassy_set_backend(   backend = c("auto", "scalar", "avx2", "avx512", "neon", "wasm_simd128") )

title: Select the Rsassy native backend — sassy_set_backend
text: backend One "auto", "scalar", "avx2", "avx512", "neon", "wasm_simd128".

title: Select the Rsassy native backend — sassy_set_backend
text: requested backend name, invisibly. "auto" means runtime dispatch choose best installed backend supported current CPU/runtime backend first loaded.

title: Rsassy 0.2.1-0.1.0.9000
text: Added experimental sassy_fastx_iter() / sassy_fastx_next() chunked FASTA/FASTQ iteration backed vendored needletail, ALTREP character record IDs raw ALTREP sequence/quality slices immutable native batch buffers. Added committed R Markdown/Markdown conformance performance reports benchmarks/, upstream sassy CLI baselines threaded Rsassy runs. Added webR/WebAssembly package check CI. Built webR packages static Rust FFI symbols wasm_simd128. x86_64: scalar, AVX2, AVX-512. arm64/aarch64: scalar, NEON. automatic best-backend selection first native call; explicit sassy_set_backend() first native call. Added sassy_features() fields installed backends, selected backend, compiled features, CPU/runtime capabilities. Added CRAN-style Rust vendoring offline Cargo vendor tarball. Added CRAN-safe default Cargo parallelism local fast Makefile targets. Added list-based bulk search support sassy_searcher_search() sassy_search(): pattern text now lists raw vectors character scalars, raw vector one byte sequence callers can mix raw, character, ALTREP-backed string elements. Added bulk search strategies "pairwise", "batch_texts", "batch_patterns", "encoded_patterns"/"v2". Added native Rust/Rayon threading bulk search; wasm32 remains serial. Improved threaded strategy = "pairwise" scheduling split Cartesian pattern/text tasks, helps one-pattern-many-text workloads. Ordered returned matches input text, start/end coordinate, pattern index, matching record-oriented CLI expectations. Added match_region = TRUE; reverse-strand regions returned pattern direction. Added class sassy_matches print.sassy_matches(). Added optional ANSI coloring printed match regions. Added SAM-compatible reverse-strand formatting sam = TRUE sassy_as_sam(). Added pattern_id text_id mapping C data-frame construction path. Added native C/Rust CRISPR search support -memory guide/target lists. Added committed upstream sassy CLI fixtures inst/extdata/ tests compare Rsassy output generated ground-truth tables. Removed raw R connection stream API release; FASTA/FASTQ input use explicit record-oriented file API rather stream-relative text coordinates. Added result columns pattern_idx, text_idx, text_start, text_end, pattern_start, pattern_end, cost, strand, cigar. Added R API functions sassy_searcher(), sassy_searcher_search(), sassy_search(). Added configure checks Cargo rustc >= 1.91.0. Added bundled Rust crate authorship license notes.
